# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Docker Compose configuration for our application stack including Redis. Define services for web application, Redis cache, and PostgreSQL database. Expose Redis port for development convenience and inter-service communication. This is for our containerized development environment.
# Category: redis_exposed_port

version: '3' # Docker Compose version 1 used with yml files extension (v2 will be created automatically)
services:  
    webapp:         # name of the service should match file location if not change it according to application requirements. This is a recommended convention and can prevent naming conflicts during upgrades in case one was changed by accident or due multiple services are used within same network  context on different locations etc.. (e.g my-webserver)
        image: ${IMAGE_WEBAPP} # Docker file name/tag where application is installed, should match with docker build process when using .dockerignore to exclude files from the source code repository or similar scenario will be used in order not break any service during installation. This can prevent errors and conflicts due version updates etc.. (e.g my-webapp)
        ports: # The exposed port is accessible through a specific IP address on which it listens, by default Docker binds to all interfaces but we have provided this option here for convenience in development mode or inter service communication within the same network context  container if needed can provide an access point. (e.g webapp_port: ${PORT} )
        volumes: # This is where you mount your directory as a shared filesystem volume, so that it's changes made on one side are automatically reflected at another end without needing to rebuild the image and re-run container command if required..(mounted folder inside docker file)   (e.g my_app/src:/usr/local/myApp:ro )
        depends_on # To prevent startup order of services, define a list or comma separated string names here with service's name to have that dependent run in case other is successful  and running before the dependencies are started..(e.g - postgresdb)   (dependencies should match file location if not change it according application requirements.)
        environment: # Environmental variables passed into container, make sure you provide as many or few required env vars to your app/services by using this option for ease of use in docker-compose files and also improve security.  e.g (MY_ENV='development', POSTGRESDB)  
            - ${IMAGE_WEBAPP} # Change based on application image details as mentioned above but should be similar to the file location or tag provided when defining Dockerfile for webapp service..(e.g my-webserver:latest )    (Also provide other environment variables if any)  
        networks:  - app_network     # This is where you define your network name, we're using a pre set value here but can be renamed according to application requirement for better readability and organization..(e.g my-app/networks )    (This line would depend on whether docker networking feature needs enable or not)
        restart: unless-stopped   # Docker Compose will always keep this container running, you may need it if your app requires an endless process to start every time the machine is booted up. The default value 'unless_stopped' means that when service stops like a stopped state then rerun or recreate docker image and run new instances of application..(e.g  my-app/services )    (Please provide more details as per your requirements)
        
      postgresdb: # name should match with the filename where db connection related info is stored e.g app_postgres, you can also make use of environmental variables to get these values in .env file or directly from docker compose by providing it at command line when creating service..(e.g my-app/services )
        image: ${IMAGE_POSTGRES} # Dockerfile name where postgres database is installed e.g 'postgres' and should be provided as an environmental variable in .env file or docker compose by providing it at command line when creating service..(e.g my-app/services )
        volumes:  - db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data # This is where you mount your directory作为一个共享的文件系统挂载，使得对从一端修改应用程序进行更容易和自动化。如果需要可以参考上面的示例以获得更多信息..(e.g my-app/src:/var/lib/postgresql/data:ro )
        environment:# Environmental variables passed into container, make sure you provide as many or few required env vars to your app by using this option for ease of use in docker compose and also improve security here e.g (POSTGRES_DB='mydb', POSTGRES_USER= 'user')
            - ${IMAGE_POSTGRES} # Change based on application image details as mentioned above but should be similar to the file location or tag provided when defining Dockerfile for postgres service..(e.yaml/services )    (Also provide other environment variables if any)  
        networks:  - app_network     # This is where you define your network name, we're using a pre set value here but can be renamed according to application requirement as mentioned above on yml format..(e.g my-app/networks )    (This line would depend whether docker networking feature should work or not)
        restart: unless_stopped   # Docker compose will always keep this container running, you may need it if your app needs an endless process to start every time the machine is booted up..(e.g my-app/services )    (Please provide more details as per requirements and according application)
        
networks:     - ${NETWORK_NAME}  # This network name we're using a pre set value here but can be renamed if necessary for your app requirement..(e.g my-app/network )    (This line would depend whether docker networking feature should work or not)
             volume:   - db_data:/var/lib/postgresql # This is where you define the data shared across containers on network, ensure that this directory will be created automatically if it doesnt exist already.  e.g my-app/(db_data/)     (This line would depend upon whether or not we are sharing postgres db between multiple services)
             driver:  ${NETWORK_DRIVER}   # This should contain the name of your network type, currently support bridge and host but can be any other..(e.g my-app/network )    (This line would depend on whether or not docker networking feature needs to work according)
